claim: AST/ALT ratio >1 indicates alcoholic hepatitis, often accompanied by hyperbilirubinemia and modest increases in alkaline phosphatase level.
Nonspecific elevations of AST and ALT may also occur in nonhepatic diseases such as cardiac or skeletal muscle damage.
